title: Epic: Structural maintainability — extract bin/forge.js into lib/ modules
description:
## Problem
bin/forge.js is very large (the single biggest file in the project) and combines setup, seed, agent fan-out, rollback, and CLI logic. Hard to change without regressions, hard to test in isolation.

## Fixes Required
1. bin/forge.js is monolithic — hard to change without regressions

## Improvements
- Technical: Extract setup, seed, agent fan-out, rollback into lib/ modules with individual tests
- Process: Smaller PRs per module after extraction

title: Fix bun hardcoded in lefthook.yml: replace bunx with npx
description: lefthook.yml commit-msg and pre-push lint hooks use 'bunx commitlint' and 'bunx eslint'. bunx is bun-only. On npm/pnpm/yarn machines these hooks silently fail or throw command not found, meaning TDD enforcement and ESLint protection are completely bypassed. Fix: replace bunx with npx --yes which works with all package managers.

title: Epic: Naming overload — three different 'validate' concepts
description:
## Problem
Three different concepts all called 'validate': (1) agent /validate slash command (workflow stage 3), (2) forge-validate CLI (bin/forge-validate.js), (3) bun run check / validate.sh (local quality gate). This causes confusion about which to use when.

## Fixes Required
1. Three concepts share the name 'validate' with no disambiguation

## Improvements
- UX: Short doc explaining: 'for prerequisites use X; for local CI-like gate use Y; in the agent use Z'
- Technical: Optional aliases (bun run validate → check) or rename one path to reduce collision

title: Epic: Maintainer scripts — sync-commands.js & check-agents.js gaps
description:
## Problem
sync-commands.js requires manual runs after editing .claude/commands — drift is possible until CI catches it. check-agents.js does not fully reflect forge setup behavior (Cursor/Codex/Cline gaps between what sync produces and what setup installs).

## Fixes Required
1. Human must remember to run sync after editing .claude/commands; drift possible until CI catches it
2. check-agents does not fully reflect forge setup behavior (Cursor/Codex/Cline gaps)

## Improvements
- Process: Pre-commit hook (optional) for sync manifest; or always run sync in a CI job on command changes
- Technical: Single pipeline for "canonical → all agents" used in both repo and setup

title: Epic: Beads & Windows — install path fragility, stats/blocked mismatch
description:
## Problem
Beads install path on Windows (npm global vs PowerShell installer) is fragile. Forge docs and research already note this. Observed possible mismatch between bd stats and bd list --status blocked counts.

## Fixes Required
1. Beads install path on Windows (npm global vs PowerShell installer) is fragile — documented in forge research but not resolved
2. Observed mismatch possible between bd stats and bd list --status blocked

## Improvements
- UX: /status and setup docs point to a single supported Windows install path
- Technical: Pin Beads version when tags exist (TODO in forge.js)

title: Add post-install verification for Beads, Lefthook, OpenSpec

title: Fix Windows Beads install: use install.ps1 instead of broken npm
description: npm install -g @beads/bd is broken on Windows due to PowerShell Expand-Archive file locking (EPERM). GitHub Issue #1031 closed as not-planned. forge.js must detect win32 and use: powershell -Command irm .../install.ps1 | iex instead. Currently forge.js prints success even when install silently failed.

title: Superpowers workflow gaps: YAGNI enforcement, /debug command, planning quality
description:
After reading actual Superpowers skill files (brainstorming, writing-plans, systematic-debugging), confirmed gaps vs our workflow:

1. YAGNI not enforced during /plan Phase 3 task writing — Superpowers brainstorming enforces 'ruthless YAGNI', writing-plans enforces 'DRY, YAGNI, TDD'. Forge has no YAGNI gate.
2. Task format missing complete code — Superpowers writing-plans requires exact file paths + line numbers + complete working code per task. Forge tasks describe work but don't include actual code.
3. No /debug command — Superpowers systematic-debugging is a full 4-phase skill with HARD-GATE: NO FIXES WITHOUT ROOT CAUSE INVESTIGATION FIRST. Forge has nothing equivalent.
4. Alternatives gate missing in /plan Phase 1 — Superpowers brainstorming step 3 requires presenting 2-3 alternatives before design approval. Forge HARD-GATE doesn't require this.
5. DRAI — user mentioned this term but it doesn't appear in any Superpowers skill file. Needs clarification from user before acting.

Research source: actual GitHub files fetched from obra/superpowers (brainstorming/SKILL.md, writing-plans/SKILL.md, systematic-debugging/SKILL.md)
notes:
Design doc at docs/plans/2026-03-02-superpowers-gaps-design.md. Branch: feat/superpowers-gaps (to be created). 4 changes: DRY gate in plan Phase 2, YAGNI filter in plan Phase 3, verification HARD-GATE in dev, /check renamed to /validate with 4-phase debug mode.
Implementation complete: 6 tasks done (0a, 0b, 1, 2, 3, 4), 0 decision gates fired. Final: 1227 pass, 0 fail. Ready for /validate.

title: Pin MCP server versions in .mcp.json.example
description: Both MCP servers use @latest: @upstash/context7-mcp@latest and @ai-tools-all/grep_app_mcp (no version). Breaking changes silently break the research workflow. Fix: pin to specific semver versions and document update process.

title: Test close flow: create then close

title: Epic: Documentation & stage naming consistency (/check→/validate, /merge→/premerge)
description:
## Problem
The project renamed /check → /validate and /merge → /premerge, but several files still use the old names, creating confusion for users and agents.

## Fixes Required
1. .cursorrules still documents /check and a flow that disagrees with AGENTS.md and docs/WORKFLOW.md
2. The hardcoded CURSOR_RULE string in bin/forge.js (shipped to Cursor users) still uses /check, /merge, and a mismatched step list
3. SKILL_CONTENT in bin/forge.js agrees with /validate + /premerge while CURSOR_RULE does not — internal contradiction in the same file
4. CLAUDE.md in the repo root still has a one-line placeholder instead of real project instructions
5. README, package.json description, and tier copy disagree on how many agents and which ones are 'supported'
6. Older docs (CHANGELOG, plan files) still mention /check as a stage name

## Improvements
- UX: One canonical stage story everywhere a customer or agent reads it
- Technical: Generate Cursor rules from the same source as canonical commands, not a hand-written string
- Process: Grep checklist or CI check for forbidden stale strings (/check as stage, /merge as stage name)

title: Epic: Reserved/dead config — _CODE_REVIEW_TOOLS unwired, false expectations
description:
## Problem
_CODE_REVIEW_TOOLS and _CODE_QUALITY_TOOLS are defined as reserved config but never wired to any behavior. Users see prompts for tools that do nothing, creating false expectations.

## Fixes Required
1. _CODE_REVIEW_TOOLS / _CODE_QUALITY_TOOLS reserved but not wired — noise and false expectations

## Improvements
- Technical: Implement minimal wiring or remove from user-facing prompts until real
- UX: Do not prompt for tools that do not change behavior

title: Fix silent skip of OpenSpec and Skills: show clear messaging
description: When OpenSpec or Skills are not pre-installed, forge.js skips them with no output. Users don't know these tools weren't set up. Fix: show explicit message: 'OpenSpec not found — install with: npm install -g @fission-ai/openspec, then re-run forge setup'

title: PR4: CLI Command Automation

Impact: High - Major DX improvement

4. Review aggregator

Plan: .claude/plans/enumerated-watching-chipmunk.md (PR4)
Enhancement needed: /status command should include intelligent stage detection (1-9) based on:
- Branch state (exists, has commits, matches PR)
- File existence (research docs, plan files, tests)
- PR state (open, reviews, approval, merge status)
- Check results (local validation, CI/CD status)
- Beads issue state (open, in_progress, blocked)

Output should clearly show:
- Current detected stage (with confidence)
- Completed checks for that stage
- Remaining tasks for stage completion
- Actionable next command

This enables true workflow automation and prevents users from getting lost.

title: Logic-level dependency detection in /plan Phase 3
description: Currently /plan Phase 3 determines issue independence based on file-level changes only. This misses logic-level dependencies: import/call chains, type/contract dependencies, and behavioral dependencies (rule changes). Add dependency analysis to /plan Phase 3 that checks these three categories before marking issues as independent.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Phase 3 analyzes import/call-chain, contract, and behavioral dependencies 2) Weighted rubric scoring proposes dependency changes 3) Detector conflicts pause for user input 4) Confidence below 70 percent escalates to the user 5) Behavioral uncertainty still proposes dependency updates for approval 6) Beads is canonical and docs are summary only 7) Approved changes use dep add, dep cycles, and graph 8) bd ready becomes more accurate 9) Use bd worktree create, JSON reads, set-state, state, and comments effectively 10) Existing tests pass or pre-existing baseline failures are explicitly separated

title: Fix global ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md — remove openspec block

Remove the OPENSPEC:START...OPENSPEC:END block entirely from ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md.

title: Epic: COMMANDS array & Claude file copy in bin/forge.js
description:
## Problem
bin/forge.js has a hardcoded COMMANDS array that lists 'check' and 'merge' (old names). The repo has validate.md, premerge.md, sonarcloud.md but missing sources are silently skipped during copyFile. Users see 'Copied: 9 workflow commands' but the actual count may differ.

## Fixes Required
1. COMMANDS array still lists check and merge; repo has validate.md, premerge.md, sonarcloud.md — missing sources copy silently via copyFile
2. Misleading 'Copied: 9 workflow commands' vs actual loop and real file set
3. No user-visible error when a command source file is missing (only DEBUG warns)

## Improvements
- UX: Fail or warn loudly when expected workflow files are not installed
- Technical: Derive the copy list from filesystem (.claude/commands/*.md) or a single manifest shared with sync, not a stale array
- Process: Automated test that the install list matches canonical commands

title: Beads-embedded plan context — auto-populate design/notes/acceptance from /plan and /dev
description:
Enhance /plan and /dev skills to automatically store plan data directly in Beads issue fields instead of relying solely on external design doc files.

**Problem:** When resuming across sessions, agents must read the Beads issue AND separately find/read the design doc. Progress state (e.g., 'task 3 of 7 done') is lost entirely between sessions.

**Solution:** Use Beads built-in fields:
- `--design`: Task breakdown/plan from /plan Phase 3
- `--acceptance`: Definition of done criteria
- `--append-notes`: Progress checkpoints as /dev completes each task
- `--description`: Keep linking to design doc, but embed key context inline

**Files to modify:**
- `.claude/commands/plan.md` — after Phase 3 task list, auto-run bd update with --design and --acceptance
- `.claude/commands/dev.md` — after each task completes, auto-run bd update --append-notes with progress

**Recovery flow after change:**
`bd show <id>` → design field has task list + notes field has progress = full context in one command

title: Epic: Setup code paths — handleSetupCommand vs interactive divergence
description:
## Problem
Two setup code paths diverge: handleSetupCommand (CLI flags) vs interactive setup. They produce different results. Two loaders (loadClaudeCommands vs loadAndSetupClaudeCommands) behave differently.

## Fixes Required
1. forge setup --agents ... path never runs setupAgent('claude') even when claude is in the list
2. Two loaders behave differently — one seeds .claude/commands when needed, the other only reads from disk

## Improvements
- UX: Same flags and interactive flow produce the same files
- Technical: One helper used everywhere — always seed .claude/commands when needed, then fan out to other agents
- Process: E2E or matrix tests for --agents combinations

title: Clean up stale workflow refs in agent commands
description:

   - Reads docs/planning/PROGRESS.md (orphaned, not part of current workflow)

2. .claude/commands/rollback.md:
   - Shows old flow: /status → /research → /plan → /dev → ...
   - References '/research payment-integration' as example

Fix both files to match current 7-stage workflow: plan → dev → validate → ship → review → premerge → verify

title: Epic: postinstall side effects — surprising file writes on npm install
description:
## Problem
postinstall in package.json runs 'node ./bin/forge.js' which triggers minimalInstall(). This causes surprising file changes and console output on every 'npm install' of the package as a dependency. Users don't expect installing a package to write files into their project.

## Fixes Required
1. postinstall runs node ./bin/forge.js → minimalInstall() — surprising file changes and console output on every install

## Improvements
- Customer UX: Document prominently on npm README; or make minimal install opt-in
- Technical: Idempotent, quiet-by-default, or scoped to when INIT_CWD looks like a real app project

title: Epic: Local quality gate — validate.sh typecheck stub & cross-platform
description:
## Problem
The local quality gate (scripts/validate.sh, bun run check) has misleading steps and cross-platform issues.

## Fixes Required
1. Step 1 calls typecheck which is only an echo — misleading 'type check passed' when no actual type checking happens
2. Script requires bash and bun; not aligned with 'Windows native' story
3. validate:yaml is Unix-style (find/grep); awkward on Windows without Git Bash

## Improvements
- UX: Honest step names or a real typecheck when/if TypeScript is added
- Technical: Optional node scripts/validate.js mirroring the bash script for cross-platform use
- Process: Document one blessed 'full local gate' command in CONTRIBUTING

title: Epic: Coverage gap — bin/forge.js excluded from c8, largest risk surface
description:
## Problem
c8 (code coverage tool) explicitly excludes bin/forge.js — the largest and riskiest surface in the project has zero coverage pressure. Changes to bin/forge.js (setup, agent fan-out, rollback) can regress silently.

## Fixes Required
1. c8 excludes bin/forge.js — largest, riskiest surface has no coverage pressure

## Improvements
- Technical: Either include it with targeted tests or document the risk as an explicit policy
- Process: If excluded, require a review checklist for changes under bin/

title: Pre-change dep guard — cross-check Beads before any dev starts work
description:
Before a developer starts any work, automatically surface open Beads issues that might be a prerequisite or conflict with what they are about to change. Prevents devs from making changes unaware of preceding/blocking work.

Problem: Developer opens a file and makes a quick change without knowing another issue is in-progress that restructures that same area. Merge conflict, rework, or logic inconsistency results.

Implementation options (to decide in /plan):
1. /plan Phase 1 HARD-GATE — before brainstorming, run: bd list --status=open|in_progress, show issues touching same feature area, ask dev to confirm no conflicts
2. Pre-commit hook — on git add of specific files, check bd list for issues owning those files (stored in issue metadata)
3. /status enhancement — when resuming work, show 'heads up: these open issues touch overlapping areas'
4. Combination: lightweight /plan check + pre-commit warning

Core behavior:
- Given files/area being changed, query Beads for open issues in same domain
- If a DEPENDS-ON chain exists (e.g. forge-ctc depends on forge-uto), warn dev if they are starting forge-ctc work before forge-uto is closed
- Surface assignee info ('Dev X is working on forge-uto which modifies bin/forge.js — you are also modifying bin/forge.js')
- Non-blocking by default (warn, don't hard-stop) unless dep issue is open and unassigned

Deliverables:
- /plan Phase 1 step: Beads cross-check before any design work
- Optionally: pre-commit hook addition to lefthook.yml
- Documentation in AGENTS.md workflow section
acceptance_criteria: 1) bd create triggers Ripple Analyst analysis 2) /plan Phase 1 surfaces overlaps 3) dep-guard.sh with find-consumers, check-ripple, store-contracts, extract-contracts 4) Contract metadata stored via --append-notes 5) LLM returns NONE/LOW/HIGH/CRITICAL + break scenarios 6) Defaults to HIGH when uncertain 7) All existing tests pass 8) Command sync works

title: Multi-developer workflow — parallel PRs, Beads coordination, dependency-aware merging
description:
Design and implement a multi-developer workflow on top of Beads that supports:

1. Issue claiming — developer assigns themselves to an issue before starting (bd update <id> --assignee=<dev>), prevents two devs picking up same issue
2. Dependency-aware PR ordering — merge order follows Beads dep chain (e.g. forge-ctc cannot merge before forge-uto)
3. Conflict zone detection — warn when two open issues touch overlapping files/areas
4. PR-to-issue linking — standardize 'Closes: forge-xxx' in PR template, verify link exists at /ship
5. Stale work detection — flag in-progress issues with no recent commits (possible abandoned work)
6. AGENTS.md / workflow doc updates — multi-dev section explaining the above conventions
7. bd ready shows who owns what so devs pick unowned work first

Deliverables:
- Workflow documentation section in AGENTS.md (or separate docs/MULTIDEV.md)
- /plan Phase 1 enhancement: show other devs' in-progress issues in same area
- /ship enhancement: verify Beads issue is linked and dependency issues are merged first
- PR template update: add Beads dep chain section

title: Epic: npm package contents vs git repo — sync/setup mismatch
description:
## Problem
The npm package (via package.json files array) ships .claude/commands but NOT the other agent trees that CI generates with sync-commands.js. Consumer install != maintainer repo. No enforced equivalence between sync --check and forge setup output.

## Fixes Required
1. package.json files array ships .claude/commands but not other agent trees that CI generates with sync
2. No enforced equivalence between sync --check and forge setup output

## Improvements
- Technical: Include generated agent dirs in the tarball OR always regenerate them in setup from one pipeline
- Process: Release checklist or CI job comparing 'setup in temp dir' vs expected manifest

title: Epic: forge CLI help & surface area — dead flags, confusing command list
description:
## Problem
forge CLI help lists --type, --interview, etc. but workflowProfiles is unused in setup — expectation gap. Workflow names (plan, dev, validate...) appear in COMMANDS but are not forge CLI subcommands — confusing for users.

## Fixes Required
1. Help lists --type, --interview, etc. while workflowProfiles is unused in setup — expectation gap
2. Workflow names (plan, dev, ...) appear in COMMANDS but are not forge subcommands — confusing

## Improvements
- UX: Help explains: slash commands = agent; CLI = setup / recommend / rollback / minimal bootstrap; points to forge-validate
- Technical: Wire --type or remove it from help until implemented

title: Epic: Non-Claude agent parity — Cursor/Codex/Cline/OpenCode setup gaps
description:
## Problem
If a user selects only non-Claude agents (Kilo, Roo, Copilot, OpenCode, etc.), .claude/commands may never be created, so converted outputs are empty — silent failure. Several agents have setup gaps vs what their plugin.json advertises.

## Fixes Required
1. Non-Claude-only selection: .claude/commands never created → converted outputs are empty (silent failure)
2. Cursor setup only writes .cursor/rules/forge-workflow.mdc; it does NOT install .cursor/commands/*.md like the synced repo
3. Codex: plugin says commands matter but setup does not emit .codex/skills/<cmd>/SKILL.md
4. Cline: no command/workflow copy from setup; only skill path — mismatch with capabilities.commands
5. OpenCode: setup path can ship full frontmatter; sync-commands.js uses keepDescription — maintainers vs consumers differ

## Improvements
- Customer UX: Any advertised agent gets the same workflow surface after setup, or docs clearly say what is manual
- Technical: Reuse adaptForAgent / sync pipeline inside forge setup, or ship pre-generated trees in the package
- Process: check-agents (or tests) assert setup output, not only repo sync

title: Epic: CI workflows — path filter gaps, mutation noise
description:
## Problem
CI workflow path filters have gaps. test.yml omits packages/** so PRs that only touch workspace packages may skip tests. Scheduled/mutation test paths need signal-vs-noise tuning.

## Fixes Required
1. test.yml path filters omit packages/** — PRs that only touch workspace packages may skip tests
2. Scheduled / mutation paths — ensure failures are noticed (noise vs signal)

## Improvements
- Technical: Add packages/** (and any other roots) to relevant path filters
- Process: Branch protection requires the same jobs contributors expect locally

title: Epic: Git hooks — lefthook lint/test divergence from package.json scripts
description:
## Problem
Pre-push lint uses 'npx --yes eslint ...'; package.json lint is 'eslint .' — these can diverge in version and behavior (network fetch vs local). Pre-push runs 'pkg test'; local habit might be only 'bun test' — mental model split.

## Fixes Required
1. Pre-push lint uses npx --yes eslint (network, possibly different version); package.json lint uses local eslint — can diverge
2. Pre-push runs 'pkg test' while developers may only run 'bun test' locally — mental model split

## Improvements
- Technical: Pre-push should call the same script as npm run lint / bun run lint where possible
- UX: Clear error messages (already partly there); ensure Windows .cmd behavior documented
- Process: CONTRIBUTING states bypasses (LEFTHOOK=0, --no-verify) and when they're acceptable
